item.dc:creator: thefixer25

item.title: My sister died today
item.description: My sister died today. I love her so much and I’m the only friend she really had in life. I feel so heartbroken that she suffered so much in her 33 years on this earth. I can only hope she has peace now and that she knew how much I love her. Her name is Chelsey. Please send her some love. -edit- I posted this because she was very alone in the world and I want people to think of her with care, in a way she deserved but rarely got. She had the biggest heart of anyone and suffered so much because of it. Thank you to everyone who said her name or held her in your thoughts, that’s all I can do for her now. She loved kitties and rubber duckies and cooking. If you want to donate in her name, an animal charity, a rehab facility, an organization that fights homelessness, or helps battered women would be good as those all describe her life. She was very silly and had a great sense of humor. She touched the people who she came in contact with. I hope for a few days her spirit lives on through all of you because god damn it life isn’t fair and she deserves better than this.
item.dc:creator: midlifecrisisapproaching

item.title: Assorted All Video Dump
item.description:
Hello-hello fellow Imgurians! My day is winding down, it was a good day overall. I hope you all had a good day, or, are about to have a good day for those of you seeing this in the morning. Check your wifi, and buckle up, because this is a pretty wild dump.
Nope rope ring
#5
This was a few months after the wildfires that ravaged our friends in Australia.
#10
Doggo yeet
#15
#20
#25 Half way point Whatcha think, nice post?
#30
Kilimanjaro lol
So. Many. Lasers.
#35
#40
#45
Hyena tax I hope you all enjoyed the dump. I appreciate you making it this far.

My brain cant take that much info o.0
Keep going!
Ill never use most of this! Jeans and white/black t-shirt works for me.
CTRL+C , CTRL+V
That was usefull for me once.
I am not that crazy about coding.
This tips works.
Just a game idea. (not mine tho)
Here is the bunny tax, Baltazar (my brother in law rabbit)

item.title: My 13th Favorite
item.description: Just be a person, people. Edit: Most Viral! Thanks all. It doesn't matter to me if this is a skit or staged, the basis of the message is what drew me to favorite this. If only people thought more of the "we" and less about "me" the world would improve vastly.
item.dc:creator: Convolutions

item.title: Ukraine, a background
item.description:
I never thought it would be possible, to hear that my people deserved genocides (yes, plural) they endured, but this is not something uncommon to hear from regular Russian when travelling. Russian and Soviet occupation decimated Ukrainians, Crimean Tatars and other native to our land nationalities, working hard and deliberately for decades to erase our identities. Poets and writers who would use their native tongue instead of language of invaders were often prosecuted. Many of them died in mysterious circumstances, many died in imprisonment.
Story of Crimean Tatars is especially tragic. After WW2 Russki decided to little more Russkificate Crimea, by getting rid from its indigenous nationality. After brief killings, soviet pigs would put any Tatar they could find on trains and dropped them in the middle of Kazakhstan prairies. Most didn't make it though first winter due to brutal conditions. This also happened to Ukrainian activists or whole villages, who dared to have an identity. In Crimea, Tatars were prohibited from owning property. Russians moved into newly vacated homes, and settled them. It is not uncommon for russki to show off rare Tatar art works (often unique styled pottery) as "family heirlooms" https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deportation_of_the_Crimean_Tatars https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1GjWlYuGg98
(on the photo, people died from starvation lining streets, because Moscow had to sell all the grain to make moneys) Holodomor is a series (!) of genocides on Ukrainian people. Russki deliberately would take away all the yield from the fields, leaving Ukrainians to starve to death. Light estimates that quarter of population was deliberately starved to death. Read that sentence twice. Ten of million of people starved to death. We believe this was to destroy national spirit and way of life. Ukraine before this atrocity was prosperous country with more individualistic outlook. Lots of quite wealthy farmer families, small businesses. This was very unlike in russia where wealth was mostly concentrated into landlord hands and peasants were working fields. Communization didn't go well due to that reasons. In Moscow decision was made to squash this spirit and show wrongness our my people ways by starving most of us to death, following by brutal repression. On my father's line only his mother survived brutalities of Russians and WW2. His father died in war fighting nazies, grandparents and cousins didn't survive the artificial famine. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holodomor Or watch "Mr Jones" 2019 Movie is about British journalists discovering the atrocity.
I don't think many of the readers actually made it here. Pretty sure only few ever had to fight for their freedom. I did, most of my friends did. We have ongoing war with Russia, almost every day causing casualties since 2014. Tens of thousands killed, millions displaced. But we will keep fighting to last drop of blood, hopefully it would be of invading troops. Fuck Putin and Russia You can often see the sentiment "Oh, but this is not Russian people, this is their government". Don't buy it. Majority of Russians support current actions. Very few oppose anything, may be corruption of Putins administration. They overwhelmingly hailed occupation of Crimea in 2014. Russians brainwashed by state media, which has even less connection to reality that mr. Trump. Russians are pumped by state crafted stories for generations. This is important thing to understand - there is no journalism in Russia. They have alternate reality in odds with news from any other source than Russian media. I am filled with anxiety. But I have full resolve to stand for Ukraine. Please, Stand with me.
item.dc:creator: svoka

item.title: I owe my life to imgur. An update on the 18 year old who imgur treated like family.
item.description:
I don't know If anyone will see this post but honestly I just want to say thank you to everyone who helped me and was the mom/parent I never had.
(My brothers apartment with his husky Syrus) I was physically and emotionally abused by my mother for the 18 years and 2 months I lived with her. I was told I was stupid, I'd never do anything, that I should never have kids and that no one loves me. I was over medicated by my mother so then I would be come as docile and dependent as she could make me. I was locked in my room for a year and a half with an alarm on the door so I could never leave, I could only leave to go to the bathroom and to go to school, she gave us a PBJ sandwich for every meal we were home for. Last September (02/21 I turned 18 4 weeks earlier) my mother was kicking me and stepping on my hands trying to hurt me. In the process of me trying to get a way she lost balance and broke her wrist. She then called the police and had me arrested and I went to jail. I got out 12ish hours later. My dad who I hadn't had a relationship for with 5ish years and had lived with for 6+ years was coming down from Idaho to pick me up. My older brother and my step mom came with him. My dad got me a hotel that night and the 3 of them drove all night from Idaho to pick me up. I was still in shock from the whole situation and couldn't stop crying. My brother and my dad picked me up from the hotel and they gave me a hug that makes made me want to not let go. We then went to pick up my step mom from her relatives house. We then drove 7 hours to boise Idaho, dropped off my brother at his apartment (I hadn't seen him for 2 years) and my step mom, dad and me went to their house. We got me signed up for school and I started going. There's stuff I should mention just incase. My step mom did not like me mainly became she was very controlling of my dad and I was kinda an outside voice talking to him. I wanted to be as nice as I could to them so I sucked up to my step mom and tried my hardest to be likeable. I cleaned their kitchen, did dinner, made jokes, etc. A few weeks pass and my dad tells me that my step mom doesn't like me and that she doesn't like that I've been going out of my way for her to like me. I was shocked and told him I didn't mean to offend her. I apologized to her the next day. 2 weeks go by and my step mom makes my dad choose me or her. My dad chose her. My brother who lives with his wife, 14 yo kid and husky in a 2 bed apartment picked me and my stuff up from my dad's house and I moved in with them. I lived on their floor for 2 weeks until I had a previous imgur post.
(The boise river that was less than a mile from my apartment) Thanks to imgur I was able to take over a lease at a shared apartment by a college. I had 1 roommate who actually gave me my first shot of whiskey (I am very light weight and almost threw it up). I lived there for 4(honestly lost track I was in a very bad mental state) and worked and rode my bike 14 miles to school. When I signed the lease I was not told I wouldn't be able to renew it, and was told I'd have to move in July. I eventually had to drop out of school because of bills I had to pay and my degraded mental health. July grew closer and closer and I was not able to find an apartment for less than $1300 (not including utilities) I was working as a chef at a retirement home at the time. July arrived and I had to move into my brother's house for 2 weeks. I had a court date In late July(or August, busy year for me) and had to go to Utah. The judge told me that i will have to pay for all the money my mom lost on wages and all of her bills/money spent due to her injury. He told me it was like $1500. When in utah I did some thinking about what I can afford and what I want in my life.
(My dad gets free standby because he works for united, the flight from Idaho to Denver) I was able to find a cheap apartment ($950 including utilities) in Ogden utah. I talked to my dad about it and we decided that because the rent was so cheap it would be good financially for me. I was able to get setup with a work from home job at Stroable as customer service representative. They have amazing benefits and provide me with wifi money and all needed equipment. In the following week I was able to get hired onto the job and get a start date. The next day after a lot of planning my dad drove me to the boise airport where I would fly on standby to Denver then to slc and have my uncle pick me up and drive me to the apartment. I looked at the recently renovated apartment and it was amazing for the price they were asking. I signed the papers later in the evening. My dad was amazing and cosigned with me. I sadly got debt because of surprise expenses the apartment required.
(The apartment I live in now) I moved into the apartment the next day with only a few pairs of clothes and a blanket. A week went by and my dad drove down with some extra house hold items for me (silverware, air mattress, cups, etc.) And he brought lots of ramen and we bought some cheap chicken. I moved to Utah about 5 months ago (I think I got that right) and I've been at the same job the entire time. I have imgur to thank for helping me when I needed help and been the support I never had. I probably forgot to say something or mention something, it has been a long year and stuff does get forgotten. Also I was in a relationship for 8 months with my girlfriend (I was gonna merry her but decided that I need to focus on me) so I haven't been alone all this time. I also did get a support animal that is making a HUGE impact on me and I literally love him to death. In short I owe imgur a lot and I am so thankful for every single one of you who has reached out to me, interacted with me, or been there when I needed it.
item.dc:creator: thecastironchef203

Map of all burger spots that are available on yelp! Big thanks to @OhBoyDoIHaveNews4U for this. Link to his post and map https://imgur.com/gallery/vcObDuD
